Visual QA (catastrophic): More than ~20% unjustified dead canvas (lower right corner, oversized QR white panel is not efficiently integrated and burns significant candidate-attention canvas).
Visual QA (catastrophic): No clear, immediate candidate-facing opportunity hook — topmost text and headline lack compelling project/destination framing.
Visual QA score 69/100 is below the 85 publishable bar

Visual QA: Agency and verification/trust elements are only minimally present and somewhat merged into the bottom block, risking confidence for Gulf recruitment (insufficient separation/emphasis for trust footer).
Visual QA: The headline 'Earning opportunity' is weak and not a strong candidate-facing hook; 'SAUDI ARABIA HOSPITALITY' is the largest but lacks recruiter urgency and opportunity framing.
Visual QA: The contact CTA, while present, does not visually pop and could be easily overlooked in dense copy.
Visual QA: More than 20% of the lower right/side canvas is underutilized by a large, stark white QR code box that detracts from the visual harmony and wastes premium canvas.
Visual QA: Job list is cramped and dense, challenging clarity at a glance, making the opportunity less instantly consumable.

requiredCorrections.note: Make the contact CTA more prominent and visually distinct for immediate action.
Spread out the job listing rows for better glance readability and less visual crowding.
Improve visual separation and typographic contrast for the verification/trust block at the bottom.
Integrate the QR code more subtly; do not use a large dead white square—overlay or badge it to preserve harmonious canvas use.
Replace the initial weak phrase with a strong candidate-facing, opportunity-centric hook that immediately conveys the project and destination.
